channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Kerb collapsed into road., 10th March
item.description:
The kerbstone and dome pavement blocks have collapsed into the road. This is restricting use of the parking bay and cars are being parked blocking private drives.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lytchet Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found, 10th March
item.description:
A Fly-tip Medium - 4 to 9 black bags problem has been found by Brent Council

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Alpha Place, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Dog Poo, 10th March
item.description:
In the last few days there is a lot of dog Poo in the common walkway areas of 1 to 17 Honiton Gardens SE15 2AT. A particular family at the rear block of Honiton Gardens walk their dog. The dog poos everywhere but the walker doesn’t pick up the poo. On 2 occasions this week a rocker has fallen and poo was on her. This is very dangerous and injurious to health. Southwark needs to do something about this as a matter of urgency
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kimberley Avenue, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed, 10th March
item.description:
Outside Marie Curie

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Service Road 1, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Caravan in the middle of an active bus lane, 10th March
item.description:
Greenwich Council and local police, do you not have means to impose law and order ?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harrow Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed vehicle, 10th March
item.description:
This vehicle is sorn
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prescott Avenue, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: People parking on pavements both sides of Dannat Close, 10th March
item.description:
People commonly park on both sides of this road, half on the pavement and half on the road. This blocks both the pavements and also the roadway - to the extent that an emergency vehicle would not be able to access the houses beyond it. This has been an issue intermittently for at least 6 months.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dannatt Close, Whetstone, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water, 10th March
item.description:
There seems to continually be water here. Possibly a leak. Needs investigating.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Lane, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Priory Gardens: Errant Drivers, 10th March
item.description:
Mon 09 Mar 2026: There really is no excuse for the way in which vehicles are driven in the park, taking short cuts and cutting corners rather than keeping to the paths, and leaving grassed areas near the Court Road gate a mud bath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Court Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 108 green lane, 10th March
item.description:
Dumped cupboard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamp post light out, 10th March
item.description:
Light out on Lamp post outside 5 Gainsborough Square
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gainsborough Square, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pile of Dog or Toxic Fox Poo on River Path, 10th March
item.description:
Pile of Dog or Toxic Fox Poo on River Path Revolting, and next to an Eatery - repugnantly Anti-Social - pile of dog-poo abandoned without any regard for the health & safety of our local community, or it could be Toxic-Fox poo which carries a whole range of pathogens such as Toxocara Canis, which can cause blindness to humans, and Canine Scabies, which can cause convulsions, blindness, paralysis and death in dogs. When will we see Enforcement Officers on duty dealing with these issues in "real time" please...?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Parade, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Danaged pavement - loose bricks, 10th March
item.description:
Please see the photo. This is house 70 or 76.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Birkbeck Road, Albany Park, Greater London

item.title: Priory Gardens: Gates Left Open, 10th March
item.description:
Mon 09 Mar 2026: Please arrange for vehicle gates to be left closed to discourage loose dogs from entering no-dogs area. TIA

item.title: Priory Gardens: Meadow Left Open, 10th March
item.description:
Mon 09 Mar 2026: Please improve signage on meadow gate to encourage visitors to close the gate which is invariably left open, and is therefore ineffective at discouraging loose dogs from entering formal gardens. TIA

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Old Priory Avenue, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
